paragraphs.text: Six emissaries from the abyssal column, sketched as luminous path-vectors. Each card is a hexagonal cell containing a single SVG drawing — teal stroke at 1.5px, radial bioluminescence — with an amber ribbon noting the species' recorded depth range.
A five-page interactive atlas of the deep ocean column — pressure, light, life, and the limits of human descent. All diagrams hand-drawn in inline SVG.

visibleText: M 0 1k 2k 3k 4k 5k 6k 7k 8k 9k 10k 11k HADAL CARTOGRAPHY Pressure Zones Creatures Light Layers Human Reach Data Dive // Atlas 02 — Bioluminescent Fauna Creatures of the dark Six emissaries from the abyssal column, sketched as luminous path-vectors. Each card is a hexagonal cell containing a single SVG drawing — teal stroke at 1.5px, radial bioluminescence — with an amber ribbon noting the species' recorded depth range. DEPTH PROFILE // 0 — 11,000 M six species · six pressure regimes · zero sunlight FIG. 02.0 Anglerfish Melanocetus johnsonii 1000–4000 M Giant Isopod Bathynomus giganteus 170–2140 M Vampire Squid Vampyroteuthis infernalis 600–1200 M Dumbo Octopus Grimpoteuthis sp. 3000–7000 M Viperfish Chauliodus sloani 200–4400 M Barreleye Macropinna microstoma 600–800 M Specimen log // recorded ranges # Common name Zone Depth (m) Pressure (atm) Bioluminescence 01 Anglerfish Bathypelagic 1000–4000 100–400 Esca lure (symbiotic) 02 Giant Isopod Bathyal 170–2140 17–214 None observed 03 Vampire Squid Bathypelagic 600–1200 60–120 Arm-tip photophores 04 Dumbo Octopus Abyssopelagic 3000–7000 300–700 Trace 05 Viperfish Mesopelagic 200–4400 20–440 Dorsal lure + belly 06 Barreleye Mesopelagic 600–800 60–80 None / passive optics Hadal Cartography A five-page interactive atlas of the deep ocean column — pressure, light, life, and the limits of human descent.
